generated_oml:
instance CentralizedDigitalTwinFramework : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwinFramework [
    base:desc "Single centralized DT Framework acting as a hub for bidirectional data exchange, coordinating between enriched BIM models and operational Digital Twins. Supports hierarchical and decentralized architecture with standardized extension points for interoperability."
]

instance DeviceSpecificDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwinInstance [
    base:desc "Digital Twin instance created by Building Control Engineers using BIM tools during deployment, tailored for device-specific modeling and control logic definition."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance OperationalDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwinInstance [
    base:desc "Digital Twin instance for Facility Managers enabling real-time monitoring and control of building operations with scoped responsibilities."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance CustomServiceDigitalTwinExtension : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwinInstance [
    base:desc "Modular, pluggable Digital Twin instance developed by DT Engineers for domain-specific extensions like analytics or custom service integrations."
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance BuildingControlSystemSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor component explicitly modeled and configured by Building Control Engineers within BIM environment as part of Building Control System (BCS) components."
    DTDFVocab:producedData BuildingControlSystemSensorData
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor capturing real-time environmental conditions (temperature, humidity, air quality) integrated into DT framework for synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionSensorData
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor monitoring structural integrity parameters (vibration, stress, deformation) for real-time building health assessment."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthMonitoringSensorData
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance EquipmentPerformanceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor tracking operational performance metrics of building systems (HVAC, lighting, elevators) for predictive maintenance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EquipmentPerformanceSensorData
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance BuildingControlSystemSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structured sensor data extracted from BIM-enriched models representing device configurations and control logic."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingControlSystemSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time environmental data collected at high frequency for synchronization between physical and digital domains."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitoringSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Structural health monitoring data transmitted with event triggers based on threshold crossings or scheduled polls."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor
]

instance EquipmentPerformanceSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Operational telemetry data from building equipment systems with normalized formats for DT integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EquipmentPerformanceSensor
]

instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Static BIM model enriched with semantic representations of sensors, actuators, and control logic by Building Control Engineers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMToDTTransformationEnabler
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance RealTimeBuildingStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated real-time data from all sensor sources representing current building state with synchronized BIM metadata."
    DTDFVocab:fromData BuildingControlSystemSensorData, EnvironmentalConditionSensorData, StructuralHealthMonitoringSensorData, EquipmentPerformanceSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo RealTimeDataProcessingEnabler
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance HistoricalBuildingPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series data archive for building operations including maintenance logs, energy consumption, and environmental conditions."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EquipmentPerformanceSensorData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance BuildingControlLogicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantic model of control logic and device dependencies automatically extracted from BIM for DT deployment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlLogicSimulationEnabler
    base:isContainedIn BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance BIMToDTTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) toolset for automated extraction of structured representations from enriched BIM models to generate deployable DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeviceSpecificDigitalTwinDeploymentService, OperationalDigitalTwinSynchronizationService
]

instance RealTimeDataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Edge computing platform with time-series database integration for low-latency processing of sensor data streams."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "AI/ML platform supporting supervised and unsupervised learning for predictive maintenance and energy efficiency analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService, EnergyEfficiencyOptimizationService
]

instance ControlLogicSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation engine for validating control logic and system dependencies before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ControlLogicValidationService, DigitalPrototypingService
]

instance CustomServiceIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Modular framework with standardized extension points for developing and integrating custom DT services and plugins."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomAnalyticsService, DomainSpecificExtensionService
]

instance DeviceSpecificDigitalTwinDeploymentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for automated deployment of device-specific Digital Twin models from BIM-enriched representations."
    DTDFVocab:provides DeviceSpecificModelDeploymentInsight
]

instance OperationalDigitalTwinSynchronizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service maintaining continuous synchronization between physical building state and operational Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSynchronizationStatusInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and processing runtime data to generate live dashboards for operators and facility managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeBuildingDashboardInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalAnomalyAlertAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using machine learning to detect anomalies in building systems and equipment performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionResultInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides AutomaticAlertTriggerAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service forecasting asset degradation and maintenance needs using historical and real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceRecommendationInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides ScheduledMaintenanceAction
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service analyzing energy consumption patterns and recommending optimization strategies."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyReportInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACOptimizationAction
]

instance ControlLogicValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating control logic changes in virtual environment before physical implementation."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlLogicValidationResultInsight
]

instance DigitalPrototypingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling disaster resilience testing and design change validation in virtual environment."
    DTDFVocab:provides DisasterResilienceAssessmentInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignChangeValidationInsight
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing domain-specific analytics through custom plugins and extensions."
    DTDFVocab:provides CustomAnalyticsResultInsight
]

instance DomainSpecificExtensionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling integration of specialized services for particular building systems or operational domains."
    DTDFVocab:provides ExtensionIntegrationStatusInsight
]

instance DeviceSpecificModelDeploymentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confirmation of successful device-specific Digital Twin model deployment with validation against BIM source."
]

instance RealTimeSynchronizationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current synchronization fidelity between physical building and Digital Twin with latency metrics."
]

instance RealTimeBuildingDashboardInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization of current building state including sensor readings, equipment status, and environmental conditions."
]

instance OperationalAnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert generation when operational parameters exceed predefined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified anomalies in building systems with confidence scores and potential root causes."
]

instance AutomaticAlertTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [

    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted maintenance needs with recommended actions, parts, and urgency levels."
]

instance ScheduledMaintenanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated scheduling of maintenance activities based on predictive analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyReportInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Detailed report on current energy consumption patterns with optimization opportunities."
]

instance HVACOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustment of HVAC systems to improve energy efficiency while maintaining comfort levels."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ControlLogicValidationResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results for control logic changes including potential conflicts and performance impacts."
]

instance DisasterResilienceAssessmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of building resilience against potential disasters with vulnerability scores."
]

instance DesignChangeValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation results for proposed design changes including structural and operational impacts."
]

instance CustomAnalyticsResultInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [

]

instance ExtensionIntegrationStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Status of custom service integrations including compatibility and performance metrics."
]

instance ApacheKafkaStreamingPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data streaming platform for high-throughput sensor data ingestion."
]

instance InfluxDBSensorDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for storing and querying high-frequency sensor data."
]

    base:desc "Machine learning framework for building predictive models for maintenance and optimization."
]

instance PyTorchAnomalyDetection : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Deep learning framework specialized in anomaly detection for building systems."

instance ANSYSStructuralSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

instance DockerContainerization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [

instance BuildingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Complete Digital Twin environment for buildings including all components and services."
    base:contains CentralizedDigitalTwinFramework,
                  DeviceSpecificDigitalTwin,
                  OperationalDigitalTwin,
                  CustomServiceDigitalTwinExtension,
                  BuildingControlSystemSensor,
                  EnvironmentalConditionSensor,
                  StructuralHealthMonitoringSensor,
                  EquipmentPerformanceSensor,
                  EnrichedBIMModel,
                  RealTimeBuildingStateData,
                  HistoricalBuildingPerformanceData,
                  BuildingControlLogicModel
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this DT system involves **control commands** generated by the digital twin and executed in the physical building infrastructure. Command types include **operational adjustments (e.g., HVAC setpoints, lighting controls, or structural monitoring triggers)** and **maintenance directives (e.g., predictive alerts or automated diagnostics)**. Control message formats are not specified but are inferred to align with **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus, or proprietary APIs) to ensure compatibility with building automation systems. Validation and authentication mechanisms are implied under the framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)**, focusing on **interoperability** and **scalability**, though specifics (e.g., digital signatures, role-based access control) are absent. Feedback systems are enabled via **bidirectional data exchange**, where physical responses (e.g., sensor confirmations or system logs) are looped back to the DT for real-time monitoring. Conflict resolution is handled through the **runtime configuration management** feature, allowing dynamic adjustments without redeployment, though explicit algorithms (e.g., priority-based arbitration) are not detailed. Command execution confirmation is ensured by the **holistic life cycle management** approach, where deployed DTs validate actions against operational constraints before physical execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **real-time data exchange capabilities** to ensure continuous synchronization between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, enabling near-instantaneous bidirectional updates. While the document does not specify exact synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz, ms), the requirement implies **low-latency operational constraints** to support dynamic monitoring and control by *Facility Managers*. The framework’s modular architecture and runtime configuration management further enable **adaptive temporal granularity** for different services, allowing adjustments without full redeployment. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to the AECO (Architecture, Engineering, Construction, Operations) domain’s need for seamless integration between static BIM models and real-time operational data, though no explicit thresholds (e.g., <100ms) are provided. The system prioritizes **operational-time-scale synchronization** over historical or predictive time scales, aligning with the life-cycle management of buildings from deployment to runtime adjustments."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system employs a **service-oriented architecture (SOA) pattern** with a **centralized yet modular orchestration hub** (the DT Framework) to coordinate heterogeneous components. Key orchestration features include:
  - **Bidirectional data flow** between enriched BIM models (static) and operational Digital Twins (dynamic), facilitated by the framework’s **standardized extension points** for service integration.
  - **Role-based component integration**:
    - *Building Control Engineers* initiate deployment via BIM tools, linking physical devices to digital models.
    - *Facility Managers* interact with real-time monitoring/control interfaces, leveraging the framework’s **immersive visualization** capabilities.
    - *DT Engineers* extend functionality through **custom services**, ensuring adaptability via runtime configuration management.
  - **Resource management** is implied through the framework’s **scalability requirements**, supporting growth from individual components to entire infrastructure systems without redeployment overhead.
  - **System-wide coordination** is achieved via the framework’s **modular architecture**, which decouples components while maintaining interoperability through standardized interfaces. The document does not specify orchestration protocols (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) but emphasizes **runtime adaptability** and **low-overhead updates** as critical to the constellation’s resilience."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in the proposed model-driven deployment framework spans from **initial device modeling** to **custom DT service development**, addressing the gap between static Building Information Modeling (BIM) representations and dynamic DT requirements. Key phases include:
  1. **Device Modeling**: Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** within the BIM environment, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages BIM’s static capabilities to model Building Control System (BCS) components.
  2. **Model Transformation**: The framework employs **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles** to extract **structured representations** from enriched BIM models, enabling **automated generation of deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention.
  3. **DT Deployment**: The generated artifacts are deployed, establishing a **real-time synchronized** connection between the physical building and its digital twin. This phase ensures **continuous synchronization** of system states.
  4. **Operation and Monitoring**: Facility Managers interact with the DT to **monitor system states** and **operate physical infrastructure**, while the DT provides **immersive visualization interfaces** for monitoring and control.
  5. **Custom Service Integration**: DT Engineers extend functionality by developing and integrating **custom services and plugins** via standardized extension points, allowing the DT to evolve with operational needs.
  Transition management between phases is facilitated by the framework’s **modular architecture**, ensuring seamless handoffs between stakeholders (e.g., Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers). Lifecycle governance is implicit in the framework’s adherence to **use-case-driven requirements** (e.g., TwinLight and BOREALIS projects) and **functional requirements (F_X)** that ensure alignment with operational and technical constraints throughout the DT’s lifecycle."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework addresses **model fidelity** by bridging the gap between **static BIM representations** and the **dynamic requirements of DTs**, ensuring that enriched BIM models (with embedded BCS components) serve as a **semantic foundation** for accurate DT deployment. **Validation and verification** are implicitly supported through the **automated generation of deployable artifacts** from structured BIM models, reducing manual errors and ensuring consistency. **Uncertainty sources** are mitigated via **real-time data exchange**, which synchronizes the physical and digital twins continuously. The framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** allow for **custom analytics and error correction mechanisms**, though explicit **quantification methods** or **quality assurance protocols** are not detailed. The system’s reliance on **MDE principles** ensures traceability and interoperability, contributing to **data integrity** across the DT life cycle."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this DT system is designed to support **heterogeneous, interoperable communication** across building systems. While specific protocols are not enumerated, the framework’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** imply compatibility with industry-standard protocols (e.g., **OPC UA for industrial IoT, MQTT for lightweight telemetry, or REST APIs for service integration**). The network topology is likely **hybrid**, combining **edge computing nodes** (for low-latency local processing) and **centralized cloud/on-premise hubs** (for global coordination). Bandwidth and latency requirements are inferred to prioritize **real-time performance**, with redundancy mechanisms (e.g., failover routing or message queuing) addressed under the **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** for **scalability and reliability**. Security protocols are not explicitly detailed but are implied to include **encryption, authentication, and access control** to safeguard data integrity and confidentiality during transmission."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this DT system supports **multi-modal hosting platforms**, including **cloud, edge, and on-premise** configurations, to accommodate varying operational needs. Computational requirements are tailored to the **modular architecture**, with **scalable microservices** handling specific functions (e.g., real-time analytics, visualization, or control logic). Storage requirements are implied to be **elastic**, scaling dynamically to manage enriched BIM models and operational data streams. Deployment models include **hybrid cloud-edge setups**, where edge nodes process time-sensitive data locally while cloud resources handle centralized analytics or long-term archiving. Availability and reliability are assured through **runtime configuration management**, enabling **low-overhead updates** and **adaptive redeployment** without full system downtime. The framework’s **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** further emphasize **maintainability** and **accessibility**, ensuring seamless integration across technical expertise levels."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin framework prioritizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** through standardized extension points and adherence to **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** for cross-system compatibility. Key integration aspects include:
  - **Data exchange protocols**: Implicit support for **bidirectional synchronization** between BIM tools (e.g., Autodesk Revit, ArchiCAD) and operational Digital Twins, though specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, REST APIs) are not mentioned.
  - **Interoperability standards**: The framework bridges static BIM representations (e.g., IFC, gbXML) with dynamic operational data, addressing **AECO domain challenges** (e.g., Section II). Integration patterns likely include **adapters or middleware** to translate between BIM schemas and real-time sensor/control data.
  - **Dependencies on external systems**: The system relies on **BIM tools for initial device modeling** and **external analytics/services** integrated via DT Engineers, suggesting **loose coupling** with third-party systems.
  - **Enterprise system integration**: While not explicitly detailed, the framework’s **scalability requirements** imply compatibility with broader enterprise systems (e.g., ERP, CMMS), though no specific integration patterns (e.g., APIs, event-driven architectures) are provided. The document emphasizes **accessibility for users with varying technical expertise**, hinting at **user-friendly integration layers** (e.g., wrappers, SDKs) for non-technical stakeholders."
]
